data.title: Italian Payments Group Nexi Appoints Bernardo Mingrone as Next CEO
data.description:
By Cristina Gallardo

Italy's Nexi named Bernardo Mingrone as chief executive officer and general manager of the group.

The payments company said that Mingrone, as deputy general manager and CEO of Nexi Payments, had acquired a deep knowledge of the group.

He will succeed Paolo Bertoluzzo, who led the group for 10 years.

The appointment comes weeks after Nexi rolled out plans to reignite growth over the next three years.

Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com

data.title: BEZ: Net profit increased to €5.2 million in 2025, with a proposed €0.11 per share dividend
data.description:
Net profit rose to €5.2 million in 2025 despite an 8% revenue decline, driven by a €4.0 million asset write-up and stable operating expenses. The equity ratio improved to 43.2%, and a €0.11 per share dividend is proposed.

data.title: Pepco Group Revenue Rises 3.7% In 25 Weeks To March 22 2026, Reaffirms FY Guidance
data.description:
Pepco Group NV :

  • FOR 25 WEEKS TO 22 MARCH 2026, PEPCO REVENUE WAS UP +4.8% ON CONSTANT CURRENCY BASIS, WITH LIKE-FOR-LIKE (LFL) REVENUE GROWTH OF +4.2% EXCLUDING FMCG

  • THIS INCLUDED POSITIVE LFL PERFORMANCE IN POLAND AND CONTINUED DOUBLE-DIGIT LFL GROWTH IN WESTERN EUROPE

  • ON A GROUP BASIS, TOTAL REVENUES WAS UP +3.7% ON A CONSTANT CURRENCY BASIS FOR THE 25 WEEKS TO 22 MARCH 2026, NEGATIVELY IMPACTED BY CONTINUED WEAKER TRADING IN DEALZ

  • WE ARE SEEING ENCOURAGING LFL PERFORMANCE OF OUR FIRST STORES IN IBERIA THAT WERE CONVERTED TO EXIT FMCG OVER A YEAR AGO

  • OVER THE SAME PERIOD, GROUP LFL REVENUES WAS UP +3.1% EXCLUDING FMCG (FLAT INCLUDING FMCG), WHILE DEALZ LFL REVENUES DECLINED BY -9.8%

  • AT THE END OF FEBRUARY 2026, PEPCO OPERATED 4,046 STORES WITH 31 NET NEW STORES OPENED IN THE H1 FY26 PERIOD TO DATE

  • PEPCO GROUP IS CURRENTLY EXPERIENCING A MINIMAL IMPACT FROM DEVELOPMENTS IN THE MIDDLE EAST

  • PEPCO TRADING PERFORMANCE OVER RECENT WEEKS HAS ACCELERATED, FOLLOWING STRONG CUSTOMER RESPONSE TO START OF SPRING/SUMMER AND EASTER COLLECTIONS, WITH LFL REVENUE GROWTH OF +9.6% EXCLUDING FMCG IN LAST FOUR WEEKS

  • THE GROUP EXPECTS TO REPORT A STRONG FIRST HALF PROFIT PERFORMANCE

  • PEPCO HAS CONTINUED TO SEE ROBUST GROSS MARGIN IMPROVEMENTS YEAR-ON-YEAR

  • FOR THE FULL YEAR, DESPITE AN UNCERTAIN CONSUMER AND GEOPOLITICAL BACKDROP, WE REMAIN CONFIDENT ON DELIVERING FY26 RESULTS IN LINE WITH THE GUIDANCE SET OUT LAST DECEMBER

  • GROUP CONTINUES TO EXPECT TO OPEN AROUND 250 NET NEW STORES ACROSS FY26, ALL FOCUSED ON PEPCO BRAND

  • AROUND 95% OF OUR IMPORTED PRODUCT VOLUME IS ROUTED VIA CAPE OF GOOD HOPE THROUGH REGULAR SEA FREIGHT UNDER CONTRACTED RATES AND CAPACITY, AND THERE HAS BEEN LITTLE DISRUPTION TO THESE ROUTE

  • BUSINESS HAS HEALTHY AVAILABILITY OF STOCK ACROSS OUR DISTRIBUTION CENTRES, PARTLY REFLECTING HIGHER LEVEL OF SHIPPED INVENTORY COMPARED TO SAME PERIOD LAST YEAR DUE TO EARLIER CHINESE NEW YEAR AND EASTER PERIOD

data.title: Tai Sin Electric Likely Positioned as Late-Cycle Beneficiary of Construction Boom — Market Talk
data.description: Tai Sin Electric is likely positioned as late-cycle beneficiary of Singapore's construction boom, CGS International analysts say in a research report. The company typically sees an increase in delivery volumes one to two years after mechanical and electrical contractors, who are its clients, win contracts as cable demand is synchronized with execution and installation phases of projects, the analysts say. The cable and wire manufacturer's production plants in Singapore allow it to secure large public-sector projects, and it could win more projects from upcoming Jurong Island green data centre park. The brokerage initiates coverage of the stock with an add rating and a target price of S$0.74. Shares are 7.0% higher at S$0.535. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Ops Retail Deems Orion Capital Subject To Rules On Transactions With Related Parties
data.description:
Ops Retail SpA upon request of Borsa Italiana SpA:

  • SAID ON WEDNESDAY COMPANY HAD CONDUCTED CHECKS OVER APPLICATION OF RULES ON RELATED-PARTY TRANSACTIONS

  • DEEMS APPROPRIATE TO SUBJECT ORION CAPITAL, RELATED RELATIONSHIPS TO RULES GOVERNING TRANSACTIONS WITH RELATED PARTIES

  • NO EVIDENCE TO SUGGEST SIGNIFICANT CONNECTION WITH GCII, WHICH REMAINS EXCLUDED FROM COMPANY'S LIST OF RELATED PARTIES

  • GCII INFORMED COMPANY IT REVOKED ORION CAPITAL'S TRUST MANDATE, WHILE IT WILL CONTINUE TO IMPLEMENT INVESTMENT AGREEMENT DIRECTLY

  • USE OF CONVERTIBLE BOND LOAN DOES NOT IN ITSELF POSE A RISK TO BUSINESS CONTINUITY, MERELY ENTAILS REWORKING OF BUSINESS PLAN

data.title: PDD Holdings Has Weaker Medium-Term Momentum — Market Talk
data.description: PDD Holdings likely has weaker medium-term momentum as growth slows and investment pressure rises, Deutsche Bank analysts say in a note. PDD's core domestic top-line growth has decelerated for seven straight quarters, while recent merchant and consumer support spending has shown limited payoff so far, it notes. The bank also expects heavier supply-chain investment and a tougher global backdrop to keep profitability under pressure, citing management's remarks. Deutsche Bank keeps a hold rating on PDD and cuts its target price to $115.00 from $126.00. The ADRs last closed at $102.61. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: ANZ Says Further Rise in Aluminium Prices Could Accelerate Demand Rationing
data.description:
ANZ:

  • SAYS WHILE HIGHER ALUMINIUM PRICES AND REGIONAL PREMIUMS HAVE ALREADY BEEN TEMPERING DEMAND, A FURTHER RISE WOULD LIKELY ACCELERATE DEMAND RATIONING

  • SAYS THEIR Q3 2026 FORECAST OF $3,600/T MARKS THE HIGH POINT IN THIS CYCLE

  • ESTIMATES NEARLY 800 - 1,000KT OF PRODUCTION WILL BE IMPACTED IN 2026 DUE TO SUPPLY OUTAGES IN MIDDLE EAST, WITH A COMPLETE RETURN TO NORMAL OUTPUT NOT EXPECTED UNTIL 2028

data.title: War in Middle East May Enter 'Talk and Fight' Phase — Market Talk
data.description: The Middle East conflict could enter a more consequential phase, that of the "dualism" of diplomacy and war, given the prospect for a restart of negotiations, Macquarie Group's Thierry Wizman says in a note. First it was about diplomacy, then it was about the war, the global foreign-exchange and rates strategist says. The phase of "dualism" may better protect market sentiment than war alone, "but it also sets us up for a greater decline if it doesn't work to provide stability and security," he says. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Switzerland's EDAG 2025 revenue falls on automotive weakness
data.description:
Overview

  • Switzerland engineering services provider's 2025 revenue declined 13% but slightly beat analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EBIT for 2025 was negative, reflecting restructuring costs and underutilization

  • Company accelerated diversification and restructuring, expects cost savings and positive EBIT in 2026

Outlook

  • EDAG expects 2026 revenue to develop within a corridor of around +/- 5%

  • Company expects a return to positive adjusted EBIT of up to around 3% in 2026

  • EDAG targets mid-term adjusted EBIT margin of 6-8% within five years

Result Drivers

  • AUTOMOTIVE WEAKNESS - Co said persistently challenging market environment in automotive industry and customer reluctance to invest led to lower revenue and order intake

  • UNDERUTILIZATION - Co said lower revenue and underutilization in mobility business drove decline in earnings in Vehicle Engineering and Electrics/Electronics segments

  • IMPAIRMENT CHARGE - Co said adjusted EBIT in Production Solutions segment was significantly impacted by EUR 15 m. impairment on a major project

Company press release:

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy", 3 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the auto, truck & motorcycle parts peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for EDAG Engineering Group AG is €6.75, about 69.2% above its March 25 closing price of €3.99

  • The stock recently traded at 12 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 21 three months ago
